title: Painter claiming injury at Bill Belichick’s house sues him over alleged unsafe work site
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Painter Andrew Jackson sues Bill Belichick for $285,436, alleging unsafe conditions at a Nantucket property led to his injury.
The incident occurred on June 21, 2024, at 45 Fair St., where Jackson claims he slipped due to plastic sheeting.
Belichick purchased the home for $4.8 million in 2024; Jackson has already received workers' compensation benefits.

title: Woman shot and killed by police in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A woman was fatally shot by police during a domestic disturbance response in Northfield, New Hampshire.
The woman's identity and the officer's name have not been released; an autopsy is scheduled.
This incident marks the third use of deadly force by police in New England since Saturday.

title: Man accused of assaulting two workers, stealing car during 3 separate trips to Dedham gas station
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: John Laurent, 35, is accused of assaulting two workers and stealing a car at a Dedham gas station.
The incidents occurred during three separate visits on Saturday at Sam's Express, 2 Washington St., Dedham.
Laurent was arrested at his West Roxbury home and faces charges including assault and vehicle theft.

title: Mass. business confidence dips back into pessimism
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts business confidence fell to 47 in March, down from 52 in February, due to energy prices and Middle East conflict.
The U.S. economy added 178,000 jobs last month, maintaining a 4.3% unemployment rate, indicating moderate growth.
Factors like volatile trade policies and geopolitical tensions contribute to caution among Massachusetts employers, despite strong labor productivity and earnings.

title: For $6.5m, this Connecticut inn comes with a liquor license in trendy Mystic
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Captain's Mansion, a boutique luxury hotel in Mystic, Connecticut, is for sale at $6.495 million.
The property includes a liquor license, furniture, fixtures, and spans 10,335 square feet with eight bedrooms.
Zoned for business and residential use, the estate features historic elements and a commercial kitchen.

title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two crew members rescued uninjured from fishing boat Legacy after it ran aground on Gloucester's rocky shore.
First responders, including police and fire departments, coordinated with the US Coast Guard for the rescue near Atlantic Road.
Rescue involved survival suits and lines due to 2 to 4-foot waves causing damage to the boat's hull.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox traded Rafael Devers to the Giants, receiving players whose contributions have largely disappointed Boston.
Boston's 2026 season start is poor, with a 2-7 record and struggles from new signing Ranger Suarez.
The Red Sox' trades involving Devers' return pieces have underperformed, highlighting management issues and past trade mistakes.

title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins have scored just three goals in their last three games, highlighting a stagnant offensive performance.
19-year-old James Hagens, currently with the Providence Bruins, could provide a potential boost for Boston's ailing offense.
Hagens' passing and power-play skills could aid Boston as they seek to secure a playoff spot with four games remaining.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: The Boston Celtics defeated the Toronto Raptors 113-95, led by Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown at TD Garden.
Neemias Queta showcased strong performance with 18 points, 7 rebounds, and 3 blocks, strengthening his case for Most Improved Player.
Nikola Vucevic returned from injury, playing 13 minutes and focusing on building chemistry ahead of the postseason.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres sentenced to 36-45 years in prison for raping a young relative hundreds of times in New Bedford.
Charges include rape of a child aggravated by age, indecent assault, and witness intimidation, Bristol District Attorney announced.
Victim reported assaults to police in 2024; offenses began when she was 12 after moving from Ecuador in 2019.
